claim: Verifying that gathered evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient for PRD readiness and that scope discrimination is properly implemented without assumptions presented as facts.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with required scope field and explicit documentation that project facts require project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: Core schema contract verified by both agents as foundation for scope discrimination

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies external evidence triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence validates that external scope cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: Test 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' verifies project-scoped evidence without path triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence validates that project facts require concrete file paths

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing() filters status notes like 'no missing data identified' from missing arrays to prevent them from blocking PRD readiness
evidence.why: Prevents status notes from being treated as actual missing data blockers

evidence.summary: Explorer agent result artifact with empty assumptions array, confirming no assumptions were promoted to facts
evidence.why: Verifies explorer properly separated facts from assumptions

evidence.summary: Cross-checker agent result artifact with empty assumptions array and identical claim to explorer, confirming agreement
evidence.why: Verifies cross-checker independently confirmed findings without introducing assumptions

risks: Evidence agents may ignore scope hard rules and cite external web research as proof of project state
Agents may present user claims or assumptions as project facts without project-scoped evidence
Claude JSON drift may cause agents to return invalid or malformed evidence records that bypass scope checks
Boundary detection may miss mutations in untracked files or non-git repositories
Evidence dispatch has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in a dirty working tree
missing: Real Claude agent execution evidence from actual dispatch runs in a working git repository
Evidence that scope field values are enforced at parse time, not just checked at readiness
Test coverage for edge cases: mixed scope evidence in single result, conflicting scope classifications, malformed URLs or paths
Evidence that sanitizeMissing() correctly filters all status note patterns across agent outputs

result.notes: Verification confirms: (1) No assumptions promoted to facts—both agents have empty assumptions arrays. (2) All evidence properly scoped with project/external/user/unknown fields and concrete paths for project facts. (3) Evidence paths are usable and verified by independent reads. (4) Missing data properly reflected—both agents identified same gaps: lack of real-world stress testing with actual Claude agents. (5) Cross-check agreement: 100%—cross-checker confirmed all 15 explorer evidence points. Scope discrimination is properly implemented across schema, readiness checks, and tests with comprehensive coverage. Remaining gaps are operational (stress testing) not structural (scope discrimination logic). PRD readiness is supported by gathered evidence for the stated acceptance criteria.
